_notes: Hand-verified part-id mappings. Verified against the V8 CAD by color-segmenting + rendering in Blender. The 8 piston crowns are confirmed (V banks visible in 3/4 view). The crank throws were CORRECTED on 2026-05-27 — the original .162/.160/.158/.156 group was at Z=0.297 (top of engine) and is actually the CAM LOBES on the cylinder heads. The real crankshaft throws are .536/.537/.538/.539 — four identical 6cm posts evenly spaced 10cm apart at the BOTTOM of the engine (Z=0.073) in the valley of the V, exactly where a crankshaft should sit. See C:/tmp/v8_seg_verify/compare_3q.png for the proof render (red=new throws, blue=pistons, green=oil pan).
verification_method: color-segment all candidates + render orthographic + visually confirm V8 layout (banks of pistons above oil pan, crankshaft posts in the V valley)

rods._reason: This CAD does not model connecting rods as separate meshes; classifier 'connecting_rod' hits are spark-plug wires / hoses. Director should narrate around this (e.g. 'the connecting rod inside the block transmits the force...') without trying to highlight a rod mesh that doesn't exist.
